title: Reland "[InstCombine] Fold (sub nuw X, (Y << nuw Z)) >>u exact Z --> (X >>u exact Z) sub nuw Y"
summary:
The generalization extends the `(X << C) | Y) >>u C` pattern to support subtraction, recognizing that both `or` and `sub` are bitwise operations that preserve low bits when shifting right. The key insight is that `(X << C) - Y` has zeros in the low C bits, allowing safe exact right shift.

The patch matches `sub nuw X, (Y << nuw Z)` with `nuw` on both operations ensuring no overflow into the low Z bits. It transforms this to `(X >>u exact Z) - Y`, propagating `nuw` and preserving `nsw` from the original subtraction. The `OneUse` check prevents regressions when the intermediate value has multiple uses.

This approach demonstrates how to generalize bitwise patterns: identify common bit-preserving properties, verify overflow flags guarantee safety, and handle multi-use cases conservatively. The test suite validates edge cases like operand order, flag variations, and extra uses.
